items.title: Trump's scandal-plagued pick wins Texas Senate primary run-off — and Democrats see an opening
Another Trump-backed candidate has won in primaries, but will his revenge tour backfire?
items.summary_auto: From a $1.8-billion fund for Donald Trump allies, which some critics call a "MAGA slush fund," to retribution for Republicans who have spoken out against him, the U.S. president is pushing his personal agenda amid declining approval ratings and the approaching midterm elections.

items.title: Jay Triano, Chief Littlechild and 1990 women's hockey team enter Canada's Sports Hall of Fame
items.summary_auto: Basketball player and coach Jay Triano, the 1990 Canadian women's hockey team and Indigenous sport leader Chief Wilton Littlechild were named the 2026 inductees into Canada's Sports Hall of Fame on Wednesday.

items.title: N.L. declares gender-based violence an epidemic
items.summary_auto: Newfoundland and Labrador's provincial government is declaring gender-based violence an epidemic.

items.title: Toronto police chief to speak with solicitor general on Ontario's police suspension laws
items.summary_auto: Toronto police Chief Myron Demkiw says he is planning to speak with Ontario's solicitor general about the restrictions chiefs face when deciding whether to suspend officers with or without pay.

items.title: Canada secures major German buyer for Ksi Lisims LNG project
German submarine bid promises Canada $86B economic boost and tens of thousands of jobs
items.summary_auto: Germany’s bid to build Canada’s next-generation submarines promises a massive economic windfall, with up to 50,000 jobs over five years and an $86-billion boost to GDP, CBC News has learned. The proposal ties Arctic defence to NATO integration, while offering major investments in shipbuilding, critical minerals, missiles, ports and energy infrastructure.

items.title: White House disputes Iranian state TV reports of terms of deal to end war
items.summary_auto: Iran's ‌state TV said it had obtained ​a draft of ​an initial unofficial framework ​for a memorandum of understanding with ‌the United States, but its report was quickly rejected as a "complete fabrication" by the White House.

items.title: Uganda orders 'immediate' closure of border with Congo as rare Ebola cases surge
items.summary_auto: Ugandan authorities on Wednesday ordered the closure of the border with Congo "with immediate effect" as suspected cases surge near 1,000 its neighbour of a rare type of Ebola and as others emerge at home.

items.title: Quebec politicians fight for right to have Canadiens watch party in the Outaouais
items.summary_auto: Provincial politicians in Quebec have unanimously backed a motion calling for the National Hockey League to allow public Montreal Canadiens watch parties in the Outaouais.

items.title: B.C. organization urges residents to report noisy boats in anticipation of federal regulation updates
items.summary_auto: Regulations on noisy boat engines and exhaust are expected, and advocates are urging people to report noisy boats this year to shape next year's enforcement.

items.title: Canada negotiating to buy Saab's GlobalEye airborne early warning aircraft
items.summary_auto: Canada has entered into negotiations with Saab to buy its GlobalEye airborne early warning aircraft, Prime Minister Mark Carney announced on Wednesday.

items.title: 5 people found alive after a week trapped in a cave in central Laos
items.summary_auto: Five villagers stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week were found alive, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are missing.

items.title: Man killed in Sask. bear attack identified as MMA fighter Hrishikesh Koloth from India
items.summary_auto: The man killed in a rare fatal bear attack in northern Saskatchewan on May 8 wanted to be an MMA fighter. Originally from Kerala, India, Hrishikesh Koloth, 27, was working at a uranium exploration site in northern Saskatchewan when he was attacked by the bear.

items.title: Man killed in bear attack identified as MMA fighter Hrishikesh Koloth from India
items.summary_auto: The man killed in a rare fatal bear attack in northern Saskatchewan on May 8 wanted to be an MMA fighter. Originally from Kerala, India, Hrishikesh Koloth, 27, was working at a uranium exploration site in northern Saskatchewan when he was attacked by the bear.

items.title: A sanctuary quietly housed lions in Ontario cottage country — then came the uproar from neighbours
items.summary_auto: Some people living near the Aspen Valley Wildlife Sanctuary in the Parry Sound, Ont., area say a compound with lions rescued from a Quebec zoo was too close for comfort last summer. The sanctuary's executive director says she wouldn't automatically rule out accepting a lion or tiger in the future, if asked.

items.title: Wildlife sanctuary, neighbours in rift over potentially housing large exotic animals near Parry Sound, Ont.
items.summary_auto: Some people living near the Aspen Valley Wildlife Sanctuary in the Parry Sound, Ont., area say a compound with lions rescued from a Quebec zoo was too close for comfort last summer. The sanctuary's executive director says she wouldn't automatically rule out accepting a lion or tiger in the future, if asked.

items.title: WW I Canadian soldier with ties to Cape Breton identified
items.summary_auto: A previously unidentified grave in northern France has been identified as that of Sgt. Norman Harold McLennan.

items.title: Canadians may ask AI for medical advice but don't want it replacing humans, poll suggests
items.summary_auto: A new poll on artificial intelligence and health care suggests that while some Canadians may be turning to chatbots for medical advice, they're far less comfortable with the technology being used to make medical decisions or replace a visit with a doctor.

items.title: Israel says it killed new Hamas military wing leader in Gaza
items.summary_auto: Dozens of Palestinians carried the body of Hamas's armed wing chief through the streets of Gaza City in a funeral procession on Wednesday, a day ‌after Israel killed him as it moves to eliminate the militant group's top brass despite a ceasefire.

items.title: N.L. justice officials can't say how many cases are tossed after trial delays. So we tracked them
items.summary_auto: The Newfoundland and Labrador Department of Justice says it does not have data on how many cases have been tossed due to R v. Jordan. So CBC manually tracked court dockets, attended hearings virtually and in person, and reviewed court audio to gather our own statistics over the past year and a half.

items.title: Upset Uber customers charged for monthly memberships they say they never signed up for
Man denied dental coverage under national plan says insurer 'playing games' with Canadians
The hot pink glow seen from space: Why these Ontario greenhouse lights still shine at night
items.summary_auto: The hot pink and canary-coloured hues glow so bright, astronauts can snap photos of them from space. Fixed atop the largest concentration of greenhouses in North America, the multi-coloured lights have transformed both the night sky in a pocket of southwestern Ontario and the province’s multi-billion-dollar agriculture sector.

items.title: Hezbollah’s cheap, easy-to-get drones have Israeli soldiers diving for cover
items.summary_auto: Israel’s government says Hezbollah’s use of small commercial drones is a tactical concern, but not a game-changer. Nonetheless,  Israel says it will escalate its attacks on Lebanese targets to try to address the growing threat.

items.title: St. Catharines, Ont., issues order to clean up abandoned, contaminated site of former GM plants
items.summary_auto: The City of St. Catharines, Ont., has issued a “repair, replace or demolish” order to owners of a long-abandoned and contaminated site that used to be two auto parts manufacturing plants once run by General Motors.

items.title: Data reveals alarming obstetric violence toward Indigenous women in Quebec
items.summary_auto: A group of researchers looking into obstetric and gynecological violence toward Indigenous women in Quebec's health-care system have released new data. That data, which comes from testimony about incidents that span from 1956 to 2023, paints an alarming picture about the different ways the bodily autonomy of Indigenous women have been violated in Quebec's health sector.

items.title: Off Campus is your new hockey romance TV obsession, and it's got a lot of Canadian connections
items.summary_auto: Heated Rivalry might have some racy competition on its hands — and the rival that's lacing up its skates is another steamy hockey streamer with a Canadian pedigree. We've highlighted some of the show's connections to the country.

items.title: #TheMoment a kid negotiated his game souvenir with an MLB player
items.summary_auto: Twelve-year-old Adam Molnar tells The National about the moment he negotiated with Esmerlyn Valdez after catching the Pittsburgh Pirates player’s first home run ball and walked away with a signed bat instead.

items.title: Joe Biden sues U.S. Justice Department over planned release of conversations with biographer
items.summary_auto: Former U.S. president Joe Biden sued the U.S. Department of Justice on Tuesday, seeking to bar the release of audio recordings and transcripts of ‌private conversations with his biographer in 2016 and 2017.

items.title: 1 dead, 9 others missing and feared dead in Washington state chemical tank implosion
items.summary_auto: A massive chemical tank holding millions of litres of a highly corrosive liquid imploded and collapsed Tuesday at a Washington paper mill, killing at least one worker and leaving nine others unaccounted for with no hope for rescue, authorities said.

items.title: Evacuation order related to overheated chemical tank near Los Angeles is fully lifted
items.summary_auto: Officials say they are lifting evacuation orders for the final 16,000 residents who live near a damaged chemical tank in California, after earlier declaring that the risk of a catastrophic explosion had largely passed.
